title: erigeron speciosus var. mollis
intro: Erigeron speciosus var. mollis, also known as the Downy Fleabane, is a flowering plant native to the western parts of North America, characterized by its delicate white or pale pink flowers and soft, downy leaves. The medicinal properties of the Downy Fleabane include its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which can help relieve coughs, colds, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of using this plant include its ability to soothe sore throats, calm digestive issues, and reduce fever, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. In herbal preparations, the Downy Fleabane can be consumed as a tea, infused in oil for topical use, or added to salves and ointments to aid in skin conditions and wound healing.
